output.summary: Accessibility audit and test page modernization for FullWidthMedia block. The block is a content-with-media type with video support (file/YouTube/Vimeo). Current implementation has several accessibility gaps: play button lacks aria-label, iframes lack title attributes, video container has no accessible description, and the section element lacks aria-labelledby. The existing test page (ID 40, slug test-full-width-media) has only 2 bare instances without DemoContainer wrappers and does not cover all attribute combinations.

fileChanges.description: Add accessibility attributes to PHP template
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-label to play button: aria-label='Play video'. 2. Add title attribute to all iframe elements (YouTube and Vimeo) for screen reader accessibility — both the initially-rendered iframe (when no poster image) and the JS-injected iframes must include a title. 3. Add aria-labelledby to the section element pointing to the heading, matching the pattern used by Hero block (auto-generated ID on section + heading). 4. Add alt='' to poster image inside play button (decorative since the button has aria-label). 5. Add aria-hidden='true' to the play icon SVG span.

fileChanges.description: Add accessibility attributes to TSX editor component
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-label='Play video' to the play button element. 2. Add aria-hidden='true' to the IconPlay span wrapper. 3. Add title attribute to YouTube/Vimeo iframes generated by playVideo(). These changes mirror the PHP accessibility improvements for editor parity.

fileChanges.description: Add title attribute to dynamically-injected iframes in frontend JS
fileChanges.details: When the play button is clicked and an iframe is injected for YouTube/Vimeo, the iframe must include a title attribute (e.g., title='Video player') for WCAG 4.1.2 compliance. Currently the innerHTML template strings create iframes without title attributes.

fileChanges.description: Replace existing test page content with comprehensive DemoContainer-wrapped instances
fileChanges.details: The current test page has only 2 instances (one image, one YouTube video) without DemoContainer wrappers. Replace with 10+ instances using takt/demo-container wrappers, covering: minimal content, full content with image, full content with video file, YouTube video with poster, YouTube video without poster, Vimeo video with poster, Vimeo video without poster, long content stress test, empty optional fields, and all content field combinations (with/without eyebrow, heading, description, buttons).

designDecisions.question: Should the section use aria-labelledby pointing to the heading, following the Hero block pattern?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes. Use auto-generated unique ID on the section and set aria-labelledby to point to the heading element. This is consistent with the Hero block's approach and provides landmark navigation for screen readers. When no heading is present, fall back to a static aria-label on the section.

designDecisions.question: What title attribute should be used for video iframes?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use 'Video player' as a generic title for embedded video iframes. The title attribute is required by WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) for iframe elements. This matches common patterns and avoids needing dynamic content.

accessibilityGaps.issue: Play button has no accessible name (no aria-label, no visible text, icon is purely visual)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-label='Play video' to the <button> element in both PHP and TSX. Add aria-hidden='true' to the IconPlay SVG/span to hide the decorative icon from screen readers.

accessibilityGaps.issue: YouTube/Vimeo iframes lack title attribute (WCAG 4.1.2 violation)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add title='Video player' to all <iframe> elements: (1) the pre-rendered iframe in PHP when no poster image is present, (2) the dynamically-injected iframes in FullWidthMedia.js (frontend), and (3) the dynamically-injected iframes in FullWidthMedia.tsx (editor playVideo function).

accessibilityGaps.issue: Section element has no accessible name or landmark identification
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-labelledby on the <section> pointing to the heading element's ID. Generate a unique ID using the same auto-ID pattern as the Hero block. When heading is not present, use aria-label with the eyebrow text or a fallback generic label.

accessibilityGaps.issue: Play icon SVG inside button is not hidden from assistive technology
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to the <span> wrapping the IconPlay SVG in both PHP (theme_block_asset) and TSX. The button's aria-label provides the accessible name, so the icon should be hidden.

accessibilityGaps.issue: Poster image inside play button may announce redundant alt text
accessibilityGaps.fix: Ensure the poster image inside the play button uses alt='' (decorative) since the button itself has aria-label='Play video'. In PHP, pass 'alt' => '' to wp_get_attachment_image. In TSX, the ImageDropUploader handles this, but verify the output image has empty alt.

accessibilityGaps.issue: Video element lacks text track/caption support notice
accessibilityGaps.fix: This is an inherent limitation of self-hosted video — editors should add captions. No code change needed, but the block documentation should note that videos with speech content need captions. Consider adding a future enhancement for text track attributes.

instances.name: Minimal - Image Only
instances.demoDescription: Only required fields (image with heading). Tests empty state handling for eyebrow, description, and buttons.

instances.name: Full Content - Image
instances.demoDescription: All fields populated with realistic content. Image media type with eyebrow, heading, description, and buttons.

instances.demoDescription: Excessive text length in all fields to test overflow and text wrapping behavior. Heading is 100+ characters, description is 500+ characters.
